result.summary: Trimble exposes HTTP APIs for Trimble Connect (authenticate with Trimble ID OAuth bearer tokens from a registered app), the Vista API (use an `X-Application-Key` API key plus subscriber code), Unity Construct (account-enabled API access key), and Geospatial Field Data/Field Configuration APIs (surface documented, but runtime auth mechanics were not confirmed).

trimble_id_oauth_app.label: Trimble ID OAuth application credentials
trimble_id_oauth_app.setup: For Trimble Connect integrations, register your app via the [Trimble Connect Integrations](https://www.trimble.com/en/products/trimble-connect/integrations) page. Trimble provides a `Consumer Key`, `Consumer secret`, `Application Name`, and `Callback Url`. Use those with Trimble ID OAuth at [Trimble Authentication docs](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication/). For user-facing apps, Trimble recommends the Authorization Code with PKCE flow; for server-to-server cases supported by a product, Trimble ID also documents a client-credentials flow at [Client Credentials](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/authentication/guides/client-credentials/).

vista_api_key.label: Trimble Vista API key
vista_api_key.setup: Get onboarded for the Vista API as described in the [Getting Started Guide](https://direct-api.xchange.trimble.com/docs/getting-started-guide). App Xchange/Vista setup is coordinated during onboarding, and your `API key` and `subscriber code` are provided during setup. For third-party access, request a vendor-specific API key during onboarding, via your customer portal, or from [App Xchange Support](https://help.trimble.com/doc/app-xchange/app-xchange/app-xchange-support).

vista_subscriber_code.label: Vista subscriber code
vista_subscriber_code.setup: Your Vista `subscriber code` is issued during Vista API onboarding alongside your API key, as described in the [Getting Started Guide](https://direct-api.xchange.trimble.com/docs/getting-started-guide). You need it together with the API key to call subscriber-scoped endpoints under the direct API path.

unity_construct_api_key.label: Trimble Unity Construct API access key
unity_construct_api_key.setup: Have a Trimble Unity Construct account enabled for API access by an account administrator. As documented in the [Unity Construct API](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/unity-construct/tools/api/v2/), once your account is enabled for API Access and you are granted an `API Access Key`, you can use the API. There is no separate special setup beyond having a configured and in-use Unity Construct instance.

geospatial_integrator_access.label: Trimble Geospatial integrator approval
geospatial_integrator_access.setup: For Geospatial Field Data services, create a Trimble Identity at [Trimble Identity](http://myprofile.trimble.com/), then complete the [Commercial Integrator Application form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeIcbea6XfYWzUIgOjDmmtDSMjXGrX5EHm3sHoBrMr73BOi4w/viewform) as described in the [Geospatial introduction](https://developer.trimble.com/docs/geospatial/). Trimble says their team will contact you about next steps. The docs do not expose the exact runtime auth binding for the APIs on the pages reviewed.
